content_en: This letter was once again filled with exhortations from the Quran and hadith. He stressed the importance of the scholars and how they are the heirs of the Prophets. He stated that many of them have families and cannot live off of just one salary. In any case, they are involved in spreading knowledge and they deserve to be supported. 2 Note that al-Nawawi, who was one of the scholars and one of the teachers, had no personal interest in this matter. He was teaching at only one school and he was not accepting his salary for teaching at that school. Hence, he was coming to the defense of his brethren scholars while not seeking any personal benefit. Al-Nawawi and Other Scholars Imam al-Nawawi had the greatest amount ofrespect for all of the other scholars throughout the history of Islam. This was very clearly shown in his writings of a biographical nature. He used to praise them for their services to Islam. At the same time, however, given the love he had for the scholars, the truth was definitely more beloved to him. He would not hesitate to point out a mistake or to disagree with a scholar if that scholar's views were not based on I Ibid., p. 39. 2 For the complete text of the letter, see ibn al-Attaar, pp. 1 15-1 16; Salmaan, al-Rudood, pp. 3 153 1 7; al-Diqr, pp. 166-168. The Life of Imam al-Nawawi sound evidence. This obviously did not mean that he did not have the greatest amount of love and respect for that scholar. Instead, he knew that it was his responsibility towards Allah to expose truth and oppose falsehood no matter what or who the source of that falsehood be. One of al-Nawawi's beloved shaikhs was al-Taaj al-Fazari. He gave a fatwa for the ruler al-Dhaahir that it was not necessary to give one-fifth of the booty for the sake of Allah, His Messenger, the poor and so forth. He tried to prove that from evidence from the Quran and sunnah. The ruler and the army were very happy with this ruling because they continued to receive large amounts of booty through their fighting. However, al-Nawawi noted the falsehood in his argument and, therefore, he could not remain silent. Al­ Nawawi stated that the shaikh had gone against the consensus of the Muslim scholars. The two debated this issue and there were harsh words between them. Al-Fazari was a better debater but al-Nawawi knew the madhab better than him, memorizing numerous texts, and he was able to confound him. He refuted him in detail and with stem words, this although he was a man who was beloved to him. But, again, Allah was more beloved to al-Nawawi.1 If al-Nawawi concluded that a person who was thought to be a scholar seemed to have a bad intention in some act he was doing, al-Nawawi would advise that person in stem words. There was a shaikh in Damascus known as ibn al-Najjaar. He tried to implement some things that went against the Quran and sunnah. He gave a fatwa (religious ruling) stating that the ruler could take the gardens from the citizens. Al-Nawawi wrote him a letter advising him to change his ways and stated that none of the earlier scholars had ever given such a fatwa. His response was to write a threatening and insulting letter back to al­ Nawawi. Al-Nawawi was forced to respond. He wrote him an extremely long letter. He kept referring to ibn al-Najjaar as "the one who wronged his own soul." He exhorted him with Quranic verses and hadith. The opening part of the letter, however, shows that al-Nawawi was not out to oppose ibn al-Najjaar until ibn al-Najjaar's own state was made very clear by his public actions. Here is the opening of that lengthy letter, All praises are due to Allah. From Yahya al-Nawawi. 0 you who are lacking in your preparation for returning to Him by leaving what is best for your own self ... I was not aware of your dislike for supporting the religion and advising the Sultan and Muslims. [I was not aware of that] because all believers should be treated with good thoughts. Every once in a while I would hear about how you were deceiving the Muslims. However, I rejected those reports with both my tongue and my heart, because such was backbiting and I did not know if those reports were true. And I continued to be in that state until these 1 For the details of that story, see al-Diqr, pp. 1 16-121. Commentary on the Forty Hadith of al-Nawawi days. [He then goes on to explain how he had lied and tried to deceive the ruler.] 1 Al-Nawawi and the Masses In sum, one can see that al-Nawawi was a defender of the masses, a defender of the scholars and a defender of the truth. He would oppose anyone who went against the Quran and sunnah. He would not just oppose the little people and leave the powers that be alone.
content_id:
Dalam surat pembelaannya untuk para ulama, al-Nawawi kembali mengisi tulisannya dengan peringatan dari Al-Quran dan hadis. Ia menekankan pentingnya kedudukan ulama sebagai pewaris para nabi, dan menyatakan bahwa banyak di antara mereka memiliki keluarga yang harus dinafkahi dan tidak bisa hidup dari satu gaji saja. Bagaimanapun juga, mereka terlibat dalam penyebaran ilmu dan layak mendapatkan dukungan. Perlu dicatat bahwa al-Nawawi sendiri — sebagai salah satu ulama dan pengajar — tidak memiliki kepentingan pribadi dalam masalah ini. Ia hanya mengajar di satu madrasah dan bahkan tidak menerima gaji dari madrasah tersebut. Dengan demikian, ia membela rekan-rekan ulama tanpa mencari manfaat apa pun untuk dirinya sendiri.

Al-Nawawi dan Ulama Lainnya: Imam al-Nawawi memiliki penghormatan yang sangat besar kepada seluruh ulama sepanjang sejarah Islam, sebagaimana tercermin jelas dalam karya-karya biografinya. Ia memuji mereka atas jasa-jasa mereka kepada Islam. Namun bersamaan dengan kecintaannya kepada para ulama, kebenaran jauh lebih dicintainya. Ia tidak segan menunjukkan kesalahan atau berbeda pendapat dengan seorang ulama jika pandangan ulama itu tidak didasarkan pada dalil yang kuat. Ini bukan berarti ia tidak mencintai dan menghormati ulama tersebut — ia tahu bahwa kewajibannya kepada Allah adalah menegakkan kebenaran dan menolak kebatilan, dari sumber mana pun.

Salah satu guru yang dicintai al-Nawawi adalah al-Taj al-Fazari. Ia mengeluarkan fatwa untuk al-Zahir bahwa tidak wajib menyerahkan seperlima ghanimah (harta rampasan perang) untuk Allah, Rasul-Nya, orang-orang miskin, dan sebagainya. Ia berusaha membuktikannya dengan dalil Al-Quran dan sunnah. Penguasa dan pasukan sangat senang dengan fatwa ini. Namun al-Nawawi melihat kekeliruan dalam argumennya dan tidak bisa berdiam diri. Al-Nawawi menyatakan bahwa sang syaikh telah menentang ijmak ulama Muslim. Keduanya berdebat dengan keras. Al-Fazari mungkin lebih unggul dalam berdebat, namun al-Nawawi lebih hafal teks-teks mazhab dan berhasil membungkamnya. Ia membantahnya secara rinci dengan kata-kata tegas — meskipun al-Fazari adalah orang yang dicintainya. Namun sekali lagi, Allah lebih dicintai oleh al-Nawawi.

Ada pula seorang syaikh di Damaskus yang dikenal dengan Ibn al-Najjar. Ia mencoba menerapkan hal-hal yang bertentangan dengan Al-Quran dan sunnah, termasuk mengeluarkan fatwa bahwa penguasa boleh mengambil kebun-kebun dari rakyat. Al-Nawawi menulis surat menasihatinya dan menyatakan bahwa tidak satu pun ulama terdahulu pernah mengeluarkan fatwa semacam itu. Balasannya adalah surat yang mengancam dan menghina al-Nawawi. Al-Nawawi terpaksa merespons dengan surat yang sangat panjang, di mana ia terus menyebut Ibn al-Najjar sebagai 'orang yang menzalimi dirinya sendiri.' Pembukaan surat panjang itu berbunyi: 'Segala puji bagi Allah. Dari Yahya al-Nawawi. Wahai orang yang lemah persiapannya untuk kembali kepada-Nya dengan meninggalkan apa yang terbaik bagi dirimu sendiri... Aku tidak mengetahui ketidaksukaanmu untuk membela agama dan menasihati sultan serta kaum Muslim. Sesekali aku mendengar tentang bagaimana engkau menipu kaum Muslim, namun aku menolak laporan-laporan itu dengan lisan dan hatiku, karena itu adalah ghibah dan aku tidak tahu apakah laporan itu benar.'

Al-Nawawi dan Rakyat: Secara keseluruhan, al-Nawawi adalah pembela rakyat, pembela ulama, dan pembela kebenaran. Ia menentang siapa pun yang menyimpang dari Al-Quran dan sunnah — ia tidak hanya berani menghadapi rakyat kecil sambil membiarkan para penguasa berlaku semena-mena.
content_ar: This letter was once again filled with exhortations from القرآن الكريم and الحديث. He stressed the importance of the العلماء and how they are the heirs of the Prophets. He stated that many of them have families and cannot live off of just one salary. In any case, they are involved in spreading knowledge and they deserve to be supported. 2 Note that النووي, who was one of the العلماء and one of the teachers, had no personal interest in this matter. He was teaching at only one school and he was not accepting his salary for teaching at that school. Hence, he was coming to the defense of his brethren العلماء while not seeking any personal benefit. النووي and Other العلماء الإمام النووي had the greatest amount ofrespect for all of the other العلماء throughout the history of الإسلام. This was very clearly shown in his writings of a biographical nature. He used to praise them for their services to الإسلام. At the same time, however, given the love he had for the العلماء, the truth was definitely more beloved to him. He would not hesitate to point out a mistake or to disagree with a scholar if that scholar's views were not based on I Ibid., p. 39. 2 For the complete text of the letter, see ibn al-Attaar, pp. 1 15-1 16; Salmaan, al-Rudood, pp. 3 153 1 7; al-Diqr, pp. 166-168. The Life of الإمام النووي sound evidence. This obviously did not mean that he did not have the greatest amount of love and respect for that scholar. Instead, he knew that it was his responsibility towards الله to expose truth and oppose falsehood no matter what or who the source of that falsehood be. One of النووي's beloved shaikhs was al-Taaj al-Fazari. He gave a الفتوى for the ruler al-Dhaahir that it was not necessary to give one-fifth of the booty for the sake of الله, His Messenger, the poor and so forth. He tried to prove that from evidence from القرآن الكريم and السنة. The ruler and the army were very happy with this ruling because they continued to receive large amounts of booty through their fighting. However, النووي noted the falsehood in his argument and, therefore, he could not remain silent. Al­ Nawawi stated that the shaikh had gone against the الإجماع of the المسلم العلماء. The two debated this issue and there were harsh words between them. Al-Fazari was a better debater but النووي knew the المذهب الفقهي better than him, memorizing numerous texts, and he was able to confound him. He refuted him in detail and with stem words, this although he was a man who was beloved to him. But, again, الله was more beloved to النووي.1 If النووي concluded that a person who was thought to be a scholar seemed to have a bad intention in some act he was doing, النووي would advise that person in stem words. There was a shaikh in Damascus known as ibn al-Najjaar. He tried to implement some things that went against القرآن الكريم and السنة. He gave a الفتوى (religious ruling) stating that the ruler could take the gardens from the citizens. النووي wrote him a letter advising him to change his ways and stated that none of the earlier العلماء had ever given such a الفتوى. His response was to write a threatening and insulting letter back to al­ Nawawi. النووي was forced to respond. He wrote him an extremely long letter. He kept referring to ibn al-Najjaar as "the one who wronged his own soul." He exhorted him with Quranic verses and الحديث. The opening part of the letter, however, shows that النووي was not out to oppose ibn al-Najjaar until ibn al-Najjaar's own state was made very clear by his public actions. Here is the opening of that lengthy letter, All praises are due to الله. From Yahya النووي. 0 you who are lacking in your preparation for returning to Him by leaving what is best for your own self ... I was not aware of your dislike for supporting the religion and advising the Sultan and المسلمون. [I was not aware of that] because all believers should be treated with good thoughts. Every once in a while I would hear about how you were deceiving the المسلمون. However, I rejected those reports with both my tongue and my heart, because such was backbiting and I did not know if those reports were true. And I continued to be in that state until these 1 For the details of that story, see al-Diqr, pp. 1 16-121. Commentary on the Forty الحديث of النووي days. [He then goes on to explain how he had lied and tried to deceive the ruler.] 1 النووي and the Masses In sum, one can see that النووي was a defender of the masses, a defender of the العلماء and a defender of the truth. He would oppose anyone who went against القرآن الكريم and السنة. He would not just oppose the little people and leave the powers that be alone.
